---
description: Produce today's video end-to-end — ground today's news, pick an angle, script, fact-check, render a FREE draft, and stop for review
---
<!-- AUTO-GENERATED from .claude/commands/daily.md by pipeline/sync_harness.py — edit the SOURCE under .claude/, then re-run `python3 pipeline/sync_harness.py sync`. Do not edit this file by hand. -->
Make today's TikiTakaFootyTV video, from today's news to a reviewable draft. Optional
starting point: $ARGUMENTS

This is the front of the pipeline (the back is `/analyze-channel`). Run it in order and
**stop at the free draft** — never spend ElevenLabs credits and never post; the owner
reviews the draft and approves production + posting separately.

1. **Pick the angle.**
   - **If `$ARGUMENTS` is given**, treat it as the topic and skip discovery.
   - **Otherwise, surface today's best 2–3 angles.** Invoke the **`soccer-news`** skill to
     refresh the current narratives (`refresh stale`, and `refresh narratives`) and read the
     hottest, most *topical* World Cup storylines for today. Lead with the channel identity —
     **daily news & stories** (a hero, an upset, a redemption arc, selection/injury drama, a
     talking point). For each candidate give: a one-line hook, the format, and why it lands
     today. **Weight by `kb/learnings.json`** — favor narrative formats (`news_story` /
     `player_story` / `shock_stat`), keep it ≤~35s, and don't pitch `quiz_top5` (it lags on
     this channel). The outlier feed (`uv run pipeline/outlier_ingest.py`) is an optional
     secondary signal for what's overperforming format-wise.
   - Use **AskUserQuestion** to let the owner choose an angle (they can also type their own).

2. **Script it.** Invoke the **`videospec`** skill on the chosen angle. It grounds via
   `soccer-news` and reads `kb/learnings.json` (step 1b) automatically, and writes
   `out/specs/<stem>.json`. Apply the veteran soccer-Shorts creator lens: a hook that wins
   the first second, an open loop closed on-screen mid-video, a real subscribe beat, and a
   seamless-loop ending (per the learnings: hook is the impression-level bottleneck AND the
   body/ending is a second leak — make both tight).

3. **Fact-check.** Invoke the **`fact-check`** skill on the new spec to verify its claims
   against the KB and current sources — especially any superlative ("first/only/most/
   youngest"). Correct in place. **Do not proceed to render with an unverified claim.**

4. **Vision-vet one-off images (only if needed).** If the spec has any `commons` scenes
   (specific real things not curated as KB entities), run
   `uv run pipeline/fetch_scene_candidates.py out/specs/<stem>.json`, then invoke the
   **`pick-images`** skill so the renderer uses the correct picture. Curated-entity-only
   specs skip this.

5. **Render a FREE draft.** `bash pipeline/make_video.sh out/specs/<stem>.json` — this is the
   default (free local Piper VO, writes `out/renders/<stem>-draft.mp4`). It auto-fetches
   images + cuts out the subject. **Never set `TTV_PRODUCTION=1` here.**

6. **Hand off for review — then STOP.** Report:
   - the chosen angle, the hook, the format, the comment-bait, and the draft path;
   - which `kb/learnings.json` findings you applied;
   - any fact-check corrections made;
   - the exact next steps for the owner (do NOT run these yourself):
     - watch the draft; if the visuals/captions/pacing are right →
       `TTV_PRODUCTION=1 bash pipeline/make_video.sh out/specs/<stem>.json` (spends ElevenLabs),
     - then post the approved render: YouTube via `uv run pipeline/upload_youtube.py upload …`
       and TikTok/IG via the **`post-social`** skill (attended).

Guardrails:
- **Draft-first, always.** Caption/layout/image fixes iterate for free in draft; only an
  approved spec gets a production run.
- **Quality bar is the owner's.** Don't declare it done — present the draft and let them judge.
- If grounding turns up nothing fresh, say so and offer an evergreen angle rather than forcing
  a weak news beat.
